titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Alan Trachtenberg Papers
titlestmt.author: Sarah Lerner

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday March 16, 2023 at 07:00

did.abstract:
Professional papers of Alan Trachtenberg, 1957-2017, that include texts and related material from conferences, lectures, and symposia, as well as writings, teaching material and course related files, correspondence, other papers and photographs, and audiovisual materials created and compiled by Trachtenberg.
Collection materials document a broad range of Trachtenberg's professional activities, including his research on the Brooklyn Bridge, daguerreotypes and photography, and Walt Whitman, as well as his time as a professor of English and American Studies at Yale University. The papers provide insight into Trachtenberg's research process, teaching methods, and approach to understanding American cultural history.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 28-31: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The Alan Trachtenberg Papers are arranged in six series: I. Conferences, Lectures, and Symposia, 1971-2013. II. Teaching Materials and Course Related Files, 1957-2013. III. Teaching Materials and Course Related Files, 1964-2013. IV. Correspondence, 1960-2017. V. Other Papers, 1973-2004. VI. Audiovisual Materials, 1973-2008.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Alan Trachtenberg (1932-2020) was the Neil Gray Jr. Professor of English and professor emeritus of American Studies at Yale University. He graduated with a Bachelor of Arts in English from Temple University in 1954 and a Master of Arts in English from the University of Connecticut in 1956. He completed his Doctor of Philosophy in American Studies at the University of Minnesota in 1962.

From 1961 to 1970, Trachtenberg taught at the Pennsylvania State University. He was a fellow at the Center for Advanced Study in the Behavioral Sciences at Stanford University from 1968 to 1969. He joined the faculty at Yale in 1969. In the 1970s, he served two terms as the American Studies department's director of graduate studies and one as department chair. He had visiting professorships in Leningrad, Kyoto, and Doshisha Universities, as well as Massachusetts Institute of Technology, Rhode Island Institute of Technology, and Cooper Union. Trachtenberg retired in 2001.

Trachtenberg's book Reading American Photographs: Images as History, Mathew Brady to Walker Evans, a study of American Photography from 1839 to 1938 (1990) won the Charles C. Eldredge Prize for outstanding scholarship in the field of American art. His other works include Brooklyn Bridge: Fact and Symbol (1965), The Incorporation of America: Culture and Society in the Gilded Age (1982), and Lincoln's Smile and Other Enigmas (2007).

Alan Trachtenberg was married to Betty Trachtenberg, who served as Yale's Dean of Student Affairs from 1987 to 2007. The two had three children: Zev Trachtenberg, Elissa Trachtenberg, and Julie Trachtenberg.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Alan Trachtenberg Papers (MS 2129). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Professional papers of Alan Trachtenberg, 1957-2017, that include texts and related material from conferences, lectures, and symposia, as well as writings, teaching material and course related files, correspondence, other papers and photographs, and audiovisual materials created and compiled by Trachtenberg.

Collection materials document a broad range of Trachtenberg's professional activities, including his research on the Brooklyn Bridge, daguerreotypes and photography, and Walt Whitman, as well as his time as a professor of English and American Studies at Yale University. The papers provide insight into Trachtenberg's research process, teaching methods, and approach to understanding American cultural history.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by Alan Trachtenberg was transferred to Yale University in 2022. These materials may be used for non-commercial purposes without seeking permission from Yale University as the copyright holder. For other uses of these materials, please contact mssa.assist@yale.edu.

Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is arranged alphabetically by conference title, organization, or host institution.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I contains printed event materials and relevant correspondence, as well as Trachtenberg's manuscript notes and typescript writings for presentations and other speaking engagements, 1971-2013.
Topics covered include the Brooklyn Bridge, cultural history, daguerreotypes, Henry Wadsworth Longfellow, Indigenous peoples, photography, Walker Evans, and Walt Whitman.

title.part: American Art of the Twentieth Century: Vision and Revision

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is arranged into four subseries: Books; Articles, Essays, and Other Writings; Book Reviews; and Writings by Others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II contains books; articles, essays, and other writings; and book reviews by Alan Trachtenberg, as well as writings by others. It is comprised of typed and handwritten manuscripts, research notes and materials, and related correspondence.

Topics covered include the Brooklyn Bridge, cultural history, daguerreotypes, Edward Weston, the Gilded Age, Lewis Hine, Abraham Lincoln, Henry Wadsworth Longfellow, Indigenous peoples, photography, Walker Evans, and Walt Whitman.

"The Presence of the Past: Photographs as Symbolic History"
"The Reader-in-the-Work: Some Observations on Melville and Whitman"
"Reading the Gilded Age City: From 'Mystery' to 'Realism'"

Winter, Oswald Le. Atoms of Memory: Poems

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series III is arranged chronologically with unidentified material filed at the end.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Folder titles assigned by Alan Trachtenberg have been retained. Supplied titles for folders received without labels are in brackets. Names of host institutions are provided in parentheses when known.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III contains syllabi, exams, lecture notes, and research materials compiled and assembled by Alan Trachtenberg while teaching at Yale University.

The earliest materials in the series relate to courses at Pennsylvania State University. Material for courses at The City of New York Graduate School and the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill are also present, as well as seminars and institutes spoonsored by the National Endowment for the Humanities.

did.unittitle: [American Studies 59b: American Literature in the Twentieth Century] (Yale University)
[English 154b: Studies in American Literature: Poe, Melville, Whitman] (Yale University)
[History 74c/American Studies 92-1c: The New World Idea in the Literature of the Americas] (Yale University)
[American Studies 600a: Readings in Cultural Criticism] (Yale University)
[English 856b/American Studies 601b: Studies in American Realism] (Yale University)

[NEH Summer Seminar: Culture and Society in the Gilded Age] (Yale University)
[American Studies 260b/English 290b: American Literature: Early 20th Century] (Yale University)
[English U 848.02: American Culture] (The City of New York Graduate School)
[American Studies 605b/English 881b: Photography and Culture in the United States] (Yale University)
[American Studies 605a: Readings in Cultural History, Theory, Criticism] (Yale University)
[American Studies 608b: Consensus and Contest: Antebellum American Writing and Culture] (Yale University)

[NEH Summer Institute: The Thirties: American Literature, Art, and Culture in Interdisciplinary Perspective] (University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill)

[Materials for photography courses taught by others]

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Correspondence with American historian Lewis Mumford--originally grouped into one folder by Trachtenberg--is filed separately.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series IV contains letters addressed to Alan Trachtenberg. Among those appearing most frequently are Lakota author Ronald Goodman, poet and photographer James H. Hiner, filmmaker Jay Leyda, historian Leo Marx, poet Robert Mezey, and photographer Jerry L. Thompson.
Letters are professional, relating to the teaching, writing, and research of Trachtenberg and his peers, as well as personal.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series V is arranged alphabetically with unidentified materials filed at the end.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series V contains notes, photographs, and miscellaneous materials created or assembled by Alan Trachtenberg.

did.unittitle: Conversations on Walker Evans (transcription from video)

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series VI is arranged by format and chronologically thereafter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series VI contains 3 reels, 9 audiocasette tapes, and 5 CD-Rs created by or relating to Alan Trachtenberg.
